yesterdayAnalysis: Rest day post-Oregon 70.3 — exactly as planned for Week 1 recovery. Zero training is intentional and correct. The 5:39 PR race on Sunday was a solid effort that exposed two critical issues: bike position causing back/foot pain on the run, and zero run calories causing the mile 10-13 fade. This recovery week exists to heal and reset before the Sacramento build begins in earnest.
todayRecommendation: Execute today's planned ROUVY bike + brick run + 3,000m pool swim at truly easy effort. Keep the bike conversational and the brick run short and loose. The swim should be aerobic form work only. If you're still sore from Sunday, cut volume — recovery is the only job this week.
weekProgress: Week 1 of 13: 0 of 250 TSS with 4 days remaining including today. This is mandatory post-race recovery — low TSS is 100% intentional and on-plan. The focus is healing, not building fitness. Once recovery is complete, the real Sacramento work begins next week.
keyInsight: You raced well on Sunday despite two fixable problems — get the bike fit scheduled immediately and drill the run nutrition protocol so Sacramento doesn't repeat Oregon's fade.
alerts: Schedule professional bike fit ASAP — back and foot pain will sabotage Sacramento training if not addressed now
SpO2 dropped to 90% on Jul 18 (race week stress) and 92% on race day — monitor trend
positives: Sleep rebounding well post-race: 84 score with 9.0h last night shows good recovery response
Oregon 70.3 PR (5:39:04) with best division placement on the run (61st) despite nutrition error — cardiovascular fitness is solid
RHR dropping back to 53 bpm after race spike to 58 — recovery progressing as expected
